cdif.bbr.metadata.ddiProperties.ddicdiDa.title: DDI-CDI Data Types
cdif.bbr.metadata.ddiProperties.ddicdiDa.description: Shared DDI-CDI structured data types from DDICDILibrary/DataTypes. Provides reusable $defs for identifiers, names, contact information, references, and other common DDI-CDI structured data types.

id-reference.description: JSON-LD @id reference to a node defined elsewhere in the graph

@id.description: IRI or blank node identifier of the referenced node

cdi:context.description: Context or usage of this name
Context or usage of this name

cdi:effectiveDates.description: Date range when this name is/was effective

cdi:isFormal.description: Whether this is the formal/legal name

cdi:typeOfOrganizationName.description: Classification of this name (e.g. legal, trade, acronym)

cdi:fullName.description: Full name as a single internationalized string

cdi:isFormal.description: Whether this is a formal name

cdi:isPreferred.description: Whether this is the preferred name

cdi:effectiveDates.description: Date range when this name is/was effective

cdi:context.description: Context or usage of this name

cdi:typeOfIndividualName.description: Classification of this name (e.g. birth, married, pen)

cdi:sex.description: Sex specification associated with this name

cdi:languageSpecificString.description: The label text with language tag

cdi:typeOfAddress.description: Type of address (e.g. home, work, mailing)

cdi:isPreferred.description: Whether this is the preferred address
Whether this is the preferred email

cdi:typeOfTelephone.description: Type of telephone (e.g. mobile, office, fax)

cdi:isPreferred.description: Whether this is the preferred telephone
Whether this is the preferred website

cdi:typeOfService.description: Type of messaging service (e.g. Slack, Teams)

cdi:isPreferred.description: Whether this is the preferred messaging service

AccessLocation.description: DDI-CDI access location for a machine (dt-AccessLocation)

cdi:mimeType.description: MIME type of the resource at this location

cdi:effectiveDates.description: Date range when this image is effective

ControlledVocabularyEntry.description: DDI-CDI controlled vocabulary entry (dt-ControlledVocabularyEntry). Allows for unstructured content which may be an entry from an externally maintained controlled vocabulary. When the content is from a controlled vocabulary, provide the code value of the entry along with a reference to the vocabulary from which the value is taken.

cdi:entryReference.description: A reference to the specific item in the vocabulary referenced by cdi:vocabulary, using a URI or other resolvable identifier.

cdi:entryValue.description: The value of the entry of the controlled vocabulary. If no controlled vocabulary is used the term is entered here and none of the properties defining the controlled vocabulary location are used.

cdi:name.description: The name of the code list (controlled vocabulary).

cdi:valueForOther.description: If the value of the string is "Other" or the equivalent from the codelist, this attribute can provide a more specific value not found in the codelist.

cdi:vocabulary.description: A reference to the external controlled vocabulary, using a URI or other resolvable identifier.

PairedControlledVocabularyEntry.description: DDI-CDI tightly-bound pair of controlled vocabulary entries (dt-PairedControlledVocabularyEntry). The extent property describes the extent to which the parent term applies for the specific case.

cdi:term.description: The term attributed to the parent class (e.g. the role of a contributor).

cdi:extent.description: Extent to which the parent term applies for the specific case. When paired with a CASRAI Contributor Role, typical values are "lead", "equal", or "supporting".

cdi:language.description: ISO language code (e.g. en, fr, de)

CorrespondenceDefinition.description: DDI-CDI CorrespondenceDefinition (dt-CorrespondenceDefinition). Describes the commonalities and differences between two members using textual descriptions plus an optional coding of the type of match.

cdi:commonality.description: A description of the common features of the two items.

cdi:commonalityCode.description: Commonality expressed as a term or code. Supports the use of an external controlled vocabulary. Ordered; repeat to cite multiple vocabularies.

cdi:difference.description: A description of the differences between the two items.

cdi:matching.description: The type of match between the two items (MatchingCriterion).

StructureSpecification.description: DDI-CDI StructureSpecification (dt-StructureSpecification). The mathematical properties of a structure — whether the member relationship is reflexive, symmetric, and/or transitive over the members in scope.

cdi:reflexive.description: Whether members are related to themselves (MemberRelationshipScope).

cdi:symmetric.description: Whether the member relationship is symmetric (MemberRelationshipScope).

cdi:transitive.description: Whether the member relationship is transitive (MemberRelationshipScope).

@type.description: Must include both schema:PropertyValue and cdi:InstanceVariable. Additional types may be included.

@id.description: reference to a skos concept for the data type

cdif:physicalDataType.description: identifier or name for the data type concept.

cdif:role.description: Specifies the role this variable plays in a data structure. UnitIdentifier names the unit a row describes; Measure holds observed/derived values; Attribute qualifies an observation; Dimension addresses a cell in a multi-dimensional cube; Descriptor names the variable that a Reference column records values for (long format); ReferenceVariable holds those recorded values.

@id.description: reference to a skos concept for the data type
reference to a skos concept for the property

cdif:uses.description: Essentially the same as schema:propertyID. References to concepts that this variable measures or represents. When the dataset's distribution carries cdi:isStructuredBy (CDIF Data Structure profile), cdif:uses references the RepresentedVariable that supplies the represented-variable-level properties below, which are then NOT duplicated on the InstanceVariable.

cdi:function.description: Immutable characteristic of the variable such as geographic designator, weight, temporal designation, etc. (InstanceVariable.function).

cdi:platformType.description: The application or technical system context in which the variable has been realized - typically a statistical processing package or processing environment (InstanceVariable.platformType).

cdi:source.description: Reference capturing provenance information for this InstanceVariable (InstanceVariable.source).

cdif:isDescribedBy_StatisticsCollection.description: The StatisticsCollection holding summary / category statistics for this InstanceVariable (InstanceVariable.isDescribedBy). cdif: namespaced and target-suffixed because the DDI-CDI isDescribedBy association is polymorphic.

cdi:hasIntendedDataType.description: The data type intended to be used by this variable, independent of its physical representation (RepresentedVariable.hasIntendedDataType).

cdi:describedUnitOfMeasure.description: The unit in which the data values are measured, expressed as a controlled-vocabulary entry (RepresentedVariable.describedUnitOfMeasure). For a plain-string unit, use cdif:simpleUnitOfMeasure instead.

cdi:qualifies.description: reference to an instance variable defined for this dataset

cdif.bbr.metadata.cdifProperties.cdifSta.title: CDIF Statistics
cdif.bbr.metadata.cdifProperties.cdifSta.description: CDIF profile of the DDI-CDI Statistics / CategoryStatistics / StatisticsCollection classes (2026-03 model). A Statistics node carries one or more Statistic value objects; CategoryStatistics carries per-category results; a StatisticsCollection groups Statistics nodes. The root validates any of the three. CDIF divergences from canonical DDI-CDI: properties valued by InternationalString / LabelForDisplay / ObjectName are simplified to plain strings and carried under the cdif: namespace (cdif:name, cdif:descriptiveText, cdif:definition, cdif:displayLabel); the polymorphic cdi:has association is split into target-specific cdif:has_* keys; cdif:appliesTo and cdif:indexedBy are CDIF additions (not present in the canonical model) that link statistics to the InstanceVariable(s) they describe.

Statistic.title: Statistic
Statistic.description: DDI-CDI Statistic dataType — a single computed value (mean, count, median, etc.). A value object with no identity; appears inline inside a cdi:statistic array.

cdi:computationBase.description: The cases included in determining the statistic — Total (all cases, valid and missing), ValidOnly (valid values only), MissingOnly (invalid cases only).

cdi:content.description: The value of the statistic expressed as a real number.

cdi:isWeighted.description: True if the statistic was computed using a weighting variable.

cdi:typeOfNumericValue.description: The type of numeric value carried in cdi:content — decimal, float, or double.

Statistics.title: Statistics
Statistics.description: A named bundle of one or more Statistic value objects for an instance variable, optionally weighted, optionally broken down by Category.

cdi:typeOfStatistic.description: Controlled-vocabulary entry naming the kind of statistic — e.g. mean, median, count, sum, stdDev.

cdi:statistic.description: Ordered list of Statistic value objects carried by this bundle. Order is significant — consumers MAY rely on array position.

cdi:hasWeight.description: The InstanceVariable whose values were used as weights when computing the Statistic entries (inline cdifInstanceVariable node or an @id-reference).

cdif:appliesTo.description: CDIF addition (not in canonical DDI-CDI): the InstanceVariable(s) this Statistics bundle summarizes — the per-bundle "what these numbers describe" link. When a Statistics node sits inside a StatisticsCollection that indexes more than one variable, cdif:appliesTo disambiguates which variable each bundle describes.

cdif:has_CategoryStatistics.description: CategoryStatistics entries breaking this Statistics bundle down by Category. cdif: namespaced and target-suffixed because the DDI-CDI cdi:has association is polymorphic.

CategoryStatistics.title: CategoryStatistics
CategoryStatistics.description: Statistics for a specific Category of an instance variable within a data set.

cdi:for.description: The Category this CategoryStatistics is for (inline Category node or an @id-reference).

cdi:typeOfStatistic.description: Controlled-vocabulary entry naming the kind of statistic.

cdi:hasWeight.description: The InstanceVariable whose values were used as weights (inline cdifInstanceVariable node or an @id-reference).

StatisticsCollection.title: StatisticsCollection
StatisticsCollection.description: Groups one or more Statistics nodes for an instance variable. A typical use is a dataset-level collection holding row-count / mean / stddev Statistics for each measured variable.

cdi:hasWeight.description: The InstanceVariable whose values were used as weights (inline cdifInstanceVariable node or an @id-reference).

cdif:has_Statistics.description: Statistics nodes carried by this collection (inline or @id-ref). cdif: namespaced and target-suffixed because the DDI-CDI cdi:has association is polymorphic.

cdif:indexedBy.description: CDIF addition (not in canonical DDI-CDI): the InstanceVariable(s) the contained Statistics index — the collection-level coordinate space.

Category.title: Category
Category.description: Minimal Category shape used as a CategoryStatistics target. CDIF simplifies the DDI-CDI InternationalString / LabelForDisplay / ObjectName valued properties to plain strings carried under the cdif: namespace. A full CDIF treatment of categories lives in cdifEnumerationDomain / SKOS Concept.

cdif:name.description: Human-understandable name(s). CDIF-simplified from DDI-CDI Category.name (ObjectName) to plain strings.

cdif:descriptiveText.description: Short natural-language account of the category. CDIF-simplified from DDI-CDI Category.descriptiveText (InternationalString).

cdif:definition.description: Natural-language definition of the category. CDIF-simplified from DDI-CDI Category.definition (InternationalString).

cdif:displayLabel.description: Human-readable display label(s). CDIF-simplified from DDI-CDI Category.displayLabel (LabelForDisplay).

SkosConcept.title: SKOS Concept (CDIF vocabulary-bound term reference)
SkosConcept.description: Vocabulary-bound term reference. CDIF policy implements the DDI-CDI ControlledVocabularyEntry / PairedControlledVocabularyEntry concept as a skos:Concept from the skosProperties building block. Under the union-type-policy a Concept value MUST be a controlled-vocabulary term — either an @id-only reference into a known scheme, a structured schema:DefinedTerm, or a full inline skos:Concept node. Plain strings are NOT permitted because vocabulary identity cannot be recovered from an unscoped string label.

cdif.bbr.metadata.schemaorgProperties.va.description: Schema.org PropertyValue-based variableMeasured. Defines properties for a measured variable in a dataset, typed as schema:PropertyValue.

schema:name.description: string label associated with the variable in the dataset serialization

items.description: human intelligible name for variable that conveys semantics

schema:measurementTechnique.description: Text description or URI specifying how values for the variable were obtained.

schema:propertyID.description: identifier or name for the property concept

schema:unitCode.description: URI or code identifying the unit of measure

schema:minValue.description: minimum numeric value in the dataset

schema:maxValue.description: maximum numeric value in the dataset

anyOf.description: link to a web page useful for interpreting the variable

cdif.bbr.metadata.schemaorgProperties.de.description:
schema.org Defined Term schema.

**Union-type policy:** In CDIF profile UML models an attribute typed as
schema:DefinedTerm is represented by a single attribute of that class type.
The JSON Schema implementation permits the property value to be EITHER a
plain string (interpreted as schema:name or schema:termCode in the default
vocabulary) OR a full schema:DefinedTerm object (with explicit @type, name,
identifier, termCode, inDefinedTermSet). Consumers should accept either form.

Note that this differs from the cdif Concept policy: a Concept (skos:Concept)
in CDIF profiles MUST be a controlled-vocabulary entry (object form or
@id-reference) — plain strings are not permitted for Concept-typed values
because vocabulary identity cannot be recovered from an unscoped label.

schema:name.description: text label for the term that is useful to human user

schema:inDefinedTermSet.description: Name for the controlled vocabulary responsible for this keyword.

schema:termCode.description: A representative code for this keyword in the controlled vocabulary. Analogous to skos:Notation

cdif.bbr.metadata.skosProperties.skosCon.title: SKOS Concept
cdif.bbr.metadata.skosProperties.skosCon.description: JSON Schema for a SKOS Concept in JSON-LD form. A unit of thought within a concept scheme. Supports labels (prefLabel, altLabel, hiddenLabel), notations, hierarchical relations (broader/narrower), associative relations (related), cross-scheme mapping properties, scheme membership, and documentary notes. Based on the W3C SKOS Reference (https://www.w3.org/TR/skos-reference/).

@context.description: JSON-LD context declaring the skos namespace prefix. May be a single object or an array of strings and objects.

skos:prefLabel.description: Preferred lexical label for this concept. A single string, a single language-tagged value, or an array of language-tagged values. Each language should appear at most once.

skos:hiddenLabel.description: Labels accessible to free-text search but not displayed.

skos:notation.description: Classification code for this concept within a scheme.

skos:definition.description: Formal explanation of the meaning of this concept.

skos:scopeNote.description: Note clarifying meaning and/or intended use.

skos:historyNote.description: Note about past state or use.

skos:example.description: Example of use of this concept.

skos:inScheme.description: Concept scheme(s) this concept belongs to.

skos:topConceptOf.description: Concept scheme(s) for which this is a top concept.

skos:broader.description: Broader (parent) concepts in the hierarchy. Items are inline concept objects or @id references.

skos:narrower.description: Narrower (child) concepts in the hierarchy. Items are inline concept objects or @id references.

skos:exactMatch.description: Concepts in other schemes with equivalent meaning.

skos:closeMatch.description: Concepts in other schemes with similar meaning.

ConceptRef.description: A reference to a SKOS Concept by URI.

LanguageTaggedValue.description: An RDF literal value with a language tag, serialized as a JSON-LD value object.

@language.description: BCP 47 language tag (e.g. en, fr, de).

@type.description: Must include both schema:PropertyValue and cdi:InstanceVariable. Additional types may be included.

@id.description: reference to a skos concept for the data type

cdif:physicalDataType.description: identifier or name for the data type concept.

cdif:role.description: Specifies the role this variable plays in a data structure. UnitIdentifier names the unit a row describes; Measure holds observed/derived values; Attribute qualifies an observation; Dimension addresses a cell in a multi-dimensional cube; Descriptor names the variable that a Reference column records values for (long format); ReferenceVariable holds those recorded values.

@id.description: reference to a skos concept for the data type
reference to a skos concept for the property

cdif:uses.description: Essentially the same as schema:propertyID. References to concepts that this variable measures or represents. When the dataset's distribution carries cdi:isStructuredBy (CDIF Data Structure profile), cdif:uses references the RepresentedVariable that supplies the represented-variable-level properties below, which are then NOT duplicated on the InstanceVariable.

cdi:function.description: Immutable characteristic of the variable such as geographic designator, weight, temporal designation, etc. (InstanceVariable.function).

cdi:platformType.description: The application or technical system context in which the variable has been realized - typically a statistical processing package or processing environment (InstanceVariable.platformType).

cdi:source.description: Reference capturing provenance information for this InstanceVariable (InstanceVariable.source).

cdif:isDescribedBy_StatisticsCollection.description: The StatisticsCollection holding summary / category statistics for this InstanceVariable (InstanceVariable.isDescribedBy). cdif: namespaced and target-suffixed because the DDI-CDI isDescribedBy association is polymorphic.

cdi:hasIntendedDataType.description: The data type intended to be used by this variable, independent of its physical representation (RepresentedVariable.hasIntendedDataType).

cdi:describedUnitOfMeasure.description: The unit in which the data values are measured, expressed as a controlled-vocabulary entry (RepresentedVariable.describedUnitOfMeasure). For a plain-string unit, use cdif:simpleUnitOfMeasure instead.

cdi:qualifies.description: reference to an instance variable defined for this dataset

cdif.bbr.metadata.cdifProperties.cdifRef.description: Building block to implement relations to other resources using a URL and some semantics.

Relation.description: DCAT Relationship surface added on top of the schema.org labeled link construct.

dcat:hadRole.description: Role of the related resource as a SKOS Concept (DCAT canonical role slot).

dcterms:relation.description: URI of the related resource (DCAT canonical pointer to what is being related to; distinct from schema:url which is the presentation URL inherited from labeledLink).

cdif.bbr.metadata.schemaorgProperties.id.description:
Properties for a schema.org identifier (schema:PropertyValue pattern).

**Union-type policy:** In CDIF profile UML models an attribute typed as
schema:Identifier / schema:PropertyValue is represented by a single attribute
of that class type. The JSON Schema implementation permits the property value
to be EITHER a plain string (interpreted as the bare identifier value) OR a
full schema:PropertyValue object (with explicit @type, propertyID, value).
Consumers should accept either form.

schema:propertyID.description: In this context for the schema:PropertyValue, this field is an identifier for the identifier schema, e.g. DOI, ARK.  Get values from https://registry.identifiers.org/registry/ for interoperability

schema:url.description: web-resolveable string for the identifier; host name part is location of a resolver that will return some representation for the given identifier value. E.g. https://doi.org/10.5066/F7VX0DMQ

cdif.bbr.metadata.schemaorgProperties.la.description: schema for a labeled link, a profile of schema.org/CreativeWork

title: DDI-CDI Physical Mapping
description: Describes how the value of an InstanceVariable is physically represented in a dataset - format, length, decimal handling, null sequences, and the W3C tabular-data-model parameters. Successor to the DDI-CDI 1.0 ValueMapping class; in the 2026-03 model it was renamed PhysicalMapping and the relationship to the variable reversed (PhysicalMapping formats InstanceVariable). PhysicalMapping has two subclasses: TextMapping (adds text-dialect detail) and LocatorMapping (adds a locator string for non-tabular physical layouts). The root validates any of the three.

PhysicalMapping.description: The base physical mapping - format and representation detail for an InstanceVariable's values in a dataset.

cdi:identifier.description: Identifier for objects requiring short- or long-lasting referencing and management.

cdi:format.description: The format of the physical representation of the value (W3C tabular-metadata format; DDI 3.2 ManagedNumericRepresentation@format).

cdi:physicalDataType.description: The base datatype of the physical representation (e.g. an integer variable stored as a floating point number).

cdi:decimalPositions.description: The number of decimal positions, used when the decimal position is implied (no decimal separator present).

cdi:scale.description: The scale of the number - a multiplier used with the value to determine the measurement.

cdi:numberPattern.description: A pattern description of the format of a numeric value (W3C tabular-metadata pattern / UAX35 number format pattern).

cdi:defaultValue.description: A default string indicating the value to substitute for an empty string.

cdi:nullSequence.description: A string indicating a null value.

cdi:isRequired.description: If True, a value is required for the referenced instance variable.

cdi:length.description: The length in characters of the physical representation of the value.

cdi:minimumLength.description: The smallest possible length of the physical representation of the value.

cdi:maximumLength.description: The largest possible length of the physical representation of the value.

cdi:position.description: The position of this mapping in an ordered sequence of mappings.

cdi:formats.description: The InstanceVariable whose values this mapping physically represents.

TextMapping.description: A PhysicalMapping for delimited or fixed-width text - adds the decimal/digit-group separators, a display label, and the field length.

cdi:identifier.description: Identifier for objects requiring short- or long-lasting referencing and management.

cdi:format.description: The format of the physical representation of the value.

cdi:physicalDataType.description: The base datatype of the physical representation.

cdi:decimalPositions.description: The number of decimal positions, used when the decimal position is implied (no decimal separator present).

cdi:numberPattern.description: A pattern description of the format of a numeric value.

cdi:defaultValue.description: A default string indicating the value to substitute for an empty string.

cdi:nullSequence.description: A string indicating a null value.

cdi:isRequired.description: If True, a value is required for the referenced instance variable.

cdi:length.description: The length in characters of the physical representation of the value.

cdi:minimumLength.description: The smallest possible length of the physical representation.

cdi:maximumLength.description: The largest possible length of the physical representation.

cdi:position.description: The position of this mapping in an ordered sequence of mappings.

cdi:defaultDecimalSeparator.description: The character separating the integer part from the fractional part of a decimal or real number. Default is "." (period).

cdi:defaultDigitGroupSeparator.description: A character separating groups of digits (for readability). Default is null.

cdi:mappingLabel.description: A human-readable display label for this mapping. Ordered; repeat for labels with different content.

cdi:formats.description: The InstanceVariable whose values this mapping physically represents.

LocatorMapping.description: A PhysicalMapping that carries a locator string addressing the value's position in a non-tabular physical layout (e.g. an XPath, JSON pointer, or array index expression).

cdi:identifier.description: Identifier for objects requiring short- or long-lasting referencing and management.

cdi:locator.description: A string that addresses the value's position in the physical layout (e.g. an XPath, JSON pointer, or array index expression).

cdi:format.description: The format of the physical representation of the value.

cdi:physicalDataType.description: The base datatype of the physical representation.

cdi:decimalPositions.description: The number of decimal positions, used when the decimal position is implied.

cdi:numberPattern.description: A pattern description of the format of a numeric value.

cdi:defaultValue.description: A default string indicating the value to substitute for an empty string.

cdi:nullSequence.description: A string indicating a null value.

cdi:isRequired.description: If True, a value is required for the referenced instance variable.

cdi:length.description: The length in characters of the physical representation of the value.

cdi:minimumLength.description: The smallest possible length of the physical representation.

cdi:maximumLength.description: The largest possible length of the physical representation.

cdi:position.description: The position of this mapping in an ordered sequence of mappings.

cdi:formats.description: The InstanceVariable whose values this mapping physically represents.

PhysicalMappingPosition.description: Assigns a position to a PhysicalMapping within an ordered sequence of mappings.

cdi:identifier.description: Identifier for objects requiring short- or long-lasting referencing and management.

cdi:value.description: Index value of the mapping in the ordered sequence.

cdi:indexes.description: The PhysicalMapping this position applies to.
